video_title: La Guerre sans armes ou Espionnage
video_subject: Feature film | World War, 1914-1918 -- France / World War, 1914-1918 -- Secret service -- Great Britain / Women and war / World War, 1914-1918 -- Belgium / EFG1914 / World War I / Belgique ; Bettignies, Louise de ; Espionnage ; Guerre 1914-1918 ; Intelligence Service ; Nord-Pas-de-Calais ; Roubaix
video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Espionage or The War Without Weapons | gênero: Feature film | duração: 00:96:00 | coleção: Maison de la Bonne Presse / Bayard Presse | direção: Choux, Jean | produtora: Exclusive Agency Films | cor: Black & White | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright / © Bayard Presse | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/31031.html | This is the romanticized story of Louise de Bettignies, who was a spy and heroin of the First World War. In October 1914, in Roubaix, Geneviève de Vandeville delivers mail in secret. Brilliant and effective, she is soon contacted by the Intelligence Service who asks her to lead a team of spies. Geneviève, who is responsible for delivering intelligence from dangerous regions, enlists the help of Maria, a young girl who becomes her friend. Maria and her lover ask Geneviève to plan their escape into Holland. This mission will prove fatal for Geneviève. She is detected by the German counterespionage, arrested, and imprisoned in Brussels. She becomes sick and dies in prison before the war ends. Some time later, Maria and her lover pay their respects to the monument in effigy of Louise de Bettignies.

Mr. Fernand David, the Minister of Agriculture, and Mr. Leullier, the Prefect of Aisne, come to thank the American Committee for its help. The land, which has lain fallow for three years, can be worked and sowed again thanks to new tractors. In the destroyed villages, all focus goes to rebuilding the homes and re-opening the schools. Equipment destroyed by the Germans is stored at the "morgue," and soldiers repair instruments for tilling. Livestock, milk, cider, poultry, and goats from other regions are distributed to the villagers. A dispensary is opened for first aid. Temporary...

An American Committee nurse, assisted by ten nurses from Bordeaux's Florence Nightingale hospital, monitor the health of five thousand children in the disaster-stricken Vic-sur-Aisne canton. The Soissons committee sends nurses to visit families living in the ruins. In Saint-Christophe, mothers bring their babies to the dispensary to have them weighed. An abundance of meals, baths, snacks, and everyday care is provided at the Boullay-Thierry convalescent home. The mayor of Soissons has asked the Committee to organize another fresh milk distribution for the public, called the "taste of milk." Trucks drive to the surrounding farms to take care of the necessary daily...

video_subject: Documentary | World War, 1914-1918 -- France / World War, 1914-1918 -- United States / World War, 1914-1918 -- Civilian relief / EFG1914 / World War I / Après-guerre; Cloche; Comité Américain; Région dévastée

The new bell was donated by the Poetry Society of America in memory of the poet Alan Seeger, who died in combat in 1916. His mother, Mrs. Charles L. Seeger, is present. The bell is hoisted up and begins to ring while a heavy rain beats down on the attendees.

Thanks to their care, infant mortality drops in the cantons under their supervision, while it increases in the rest of the department. The nurses visit children who are injured or have tuberculosis and, along with the committee, organize their convalescent care in Swiss families. video_title: Nos troupes dans les pays rhénans
video_subject: Documentary | World War, 1914-1918 -- France / Military occupation / World War, 1914-1918 -- Armistices / Soldiers / EFG1914 / World War I / Après-guerre (1918-1925) ; Armistice ; Douane ; Soldat
video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Troops in Rhenish Territory | gênero: Documentary | duração: 00:28:02 | coleção: Albert-Kahn musée et jardin départementaux - Département des Hauts-de-Seine | cor: Black & White | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright / Albert-Kahn musée et jardin départementaux - Département des Hauts-de-Seine | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/K_77174.html | In 1921, following the World War I armistice, the Allies occupy a section of German territory in order to obtain war reparations. The French troops are situated in the Rhenish territory, whose first French customs post is located at Gerresheim. The agreement with the civilians seems cordial: the soldiers check a few vehicles and distribute candy to children. The review of other customs offices is an opportunity to see Duisburg, the Ruhr bridge, the Düsseldorf windmills, and the Homberg bridge.

video_title: Recreation as a Part of Reconstruction
video_subject: Documentary | World War, 1914-1918 -- France / World War, 1914-1918 -- United States / World War, 1914-1918 -- Civilian relief / EFG1914 / World War I / Après-guerre; Comité Américain; Culture physique; Rugby; Stade
video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: La Récréation fait partie de la reconstruction | gênero: Documentary | duração: 00:14:24 | coleção: Musée franco-américain du Château de Blérancourt | direção: Tyree, Millard | produtora: American Committee | cor: Black & White | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright / Musée franco-américain du Château de Blérancourt | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/147943.html | After having created laws on practicing physical culture, the government, in collaboration with the American Committee, brings in American experts who adapt their principles to the French system. In July 1921, schools in the Coucy-le-Château canton compete in physical culture competitions organized in Blérancourt. In Saint-Crépin, the land acquired for building a stadium is set up before the first stone is placed. At the event, the first rugby match is played since the war. Basketball games are organized at Vic-sur-Aisne and Crouy.

video_subject: n/a | World War, 1914-1918 -- France / Weapons industry / EFG1914 / World War I / Char d'assaut ; Equipement militaire ; Guerre 1914-1918
video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Schneider Tanks | gênero: n/a | duração: 00:13:18 | coleção: Ecomusée Creusot Montceau | direção: Unknown | produtora: Etablissements Schneider ? | cor: Black & White | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright / Ecomusée Creusot Montceau | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/131851.html | It is September 1915, and the Schneider company is manufacturing a tank with crawler tracks, designed in collaboration with Colonel Estienne. On February 25, 1916, 400 tanks are ordered. The construction and assembly are carried out in SOMUA workshops, in Saint-Ouen. These tanks are equipped with a short 75 mm canon, two machine guns, and have 60 horsepower engines, which allow them to reach 8 km/hr. The tank's interior has two driver seats and can hold a seven-person crew. Transported to the French front by train or trailer, these tanks stood out in combat by maneuvering in coordination with the infantry in 1917 and 1918.

video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Scenes of the City | gênero: Documentary | duração: 00:24:51 | coleção: Albert-Kahn musée et jardin départementaux - Département des Hauts-de-Seine | direção: Unknown | cor: Black & White | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright / Albert-Kahn musée et jardin départementaux - Département des Hauts-de-Seine | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/K_79216.html | In August 1918, during World War I, the city of Havre is a swarm of activity. The naval base of the Belgian, British, and American forces, many soldiers transit through this city. At the port, shuttles arrive and unload their goods, sea planes land and take off, and war prisoners work on construction projects. video_subject: Short film | World War, 1914-1918 -- France / Savings bonds / EFG1914 / World War I / Emprunt national ; France ; Guerre ; Guerre 1914-1918
video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: The Handkerchief | gênero: Short film | duração: 00:14:34 | coleção: Musée Ecole de la Perrine | direção: Unknown | produtora: Société des Etablissements Gaumont | cor: Tinted / Toned / Hand coloured | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright / Musée Ecole de la Perrine | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/131887.html | Lefrancois, a father of six, is a soldier. His seventh child is on the way and will be named Victory. Lefrancois cannot remember why he tied a knot in his handkerchief. Although he has received the War Cross for capturing German soldiers, all he can think about is the gap in his memory. The next day, he is hit by a shell and transported to a hospital, where he remains obsessed with his forgetfulness. A friend pays him a visit and accuses him of looking emprunté (ill-at-ease). As soon as he hears the word emprunté, his memory comes back to him: he must tell his wife to subscribe to the emprunt national (national loan). Back on his feet, he asks viewers, "Have you subscribed?"

video_subject: Documentary | World War, 1914-1918 -- France / City and town life / EFG1914 / World War I / Arme ; Cérémonie officielle ; Exposition ; Guerre 1914-1918 ; Paris
video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Drahen Balloons, Trophies and Map of the Front | gênero: Documentary | duração: 00:07:00 | coleção: Albert-Kahn musée et jardin départementaux - Département des Hauts-de-Seine | cor: Black & White | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright / Albert-Kahn musée et jardin départementaux - Département des Hauts-de-Seine | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/K_79120.html | Paris, in September and October 1918. Captured Drahen balloons are displayed in the Tuileries Garden and on the Champs-Elysées. On the boulevards, Parisians can see the advances on the western front thanks to a map displayed by the newspaper Le Quotidien. In a park, children navigate their sailboats in a pond. At Place de la Concorde, a tank shell, canons, mortars, and howitzers are some of the captured German artillery pieces displayed in public as war trophies. The English general Robert Baden-Powell presents a flag to Boy Scouts.

video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Uncle Sam | gênero: Animated Film | duração: 00:02:43 | coleção: Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - CNC << Back to search results | direção: Unknown | cor: Tinted / Toned / Hand coloured | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/143873.html | A man is drawing Uncle Sam sitting on a pile of bags filled with dollar bills. video_subject: Short film | World War, 1914-1918 -- France / World War, 1914-1918 -- Motion pictures and the war / Civilians in war / Children and war / EFG1914 / World War I / Alsace ; Enfant ; Espionnage ; Guerre 1914-1918
video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: What They Did | gênero: Short film | duração: 00:33:35 | coleção: Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - CNC | direção: Unknown | cor: Tinted / Toned / Hand coloured | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/105041.html | Jean and Suzette, two orphans, are raised by the inn-keeper Frantz Hobscher who mistreats them. When World War I is declared, the imperial authorities order Hobscher to trick spies into thinking he's Alsatian. The children's protector, Jacques Morel, is sent to the front. Some time later, the young man stops at the village, carrying a very important military letter. So, Hobscher decides to seize the letter and shuts the young man in the cellar. However, Jean is able to get into the cellar and Jacques tasks him with delivering the letter to the French command. While being pursued by Hobscher, Jean is taken in by French soldiers. The spy is unmasked and, a few months later, Jean receives honors for his courage.

video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Manufacturing munitions and war materials | gênero: Adverstising film | duração: 00:08:50 | coleção: Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - CNC | direção: Unknown | produtora: Société des Etablissements Gaumont | cor: Tinted / Toned / Hand coloured | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/26017.html | During the First World War, Vickers manufactured munitions and war materials needed for combat. In the workshops, shrapnel and shells are manufactured by workers, while women and young girls assemble the parts and load the shells with powder. When the work is completed, armored trucks transport the munitions to their destination. At the dockyards, a warship is constructed using steel plates and pieces cut from armored vehicles using acetylene flames.

"From Domrémy to Chinon." Joan the Maid, guided by heavenly voices, must convince others of her pre-destined role and aptitude to command the French Army. She leaves her native village to speak to the king, who, after numerous incidents, agrees to leave his troops in her command. "The Liberation of Orléans." Battles against the English establish Joan's authority in this exclusively masculine world. The recapture of the towers of Orléans symbolizes the victory. "Hour of glory...hour of suffering." video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Harry News 1915 | gênero: newsreel | duração: 00:04:25 | coleção: Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - CNC | direção: Unknown | produtora: Harry-Journal | cor: Black & White | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/112644.html | Eight topics of the times: - the British naval fusiliers leave for Dardanelles. -British Red Cross Day. -In Arrow, at the Officers School, Colonel Herro is decorated for acts of glory in the face of the enemy. -In Clapton, a battalion made up exclusively of soccer players organizes a match. - In Nish, the flags of the regiments leaving for the front receive a blessing. - In Rome, the crowd applauds the royal family when they appear at the Quirinal balcony. - At Woodrow Wilson's camp, the young American army performs drills in front of a large crowd. -In Westende, a Tommy, newly returned from the front, gets clean by taking a dip in the sea.

video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: The French Air Force on the Front | gênero: Newsreel | duração: 00:09:05 | coleção: Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - CNC | direção: Unknown | produtora: SCA - Section Cinématographique des Armées | cor: Tinted / Toned / Hand coloured | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/178598.html | The year is 1915. The French air force, fighting on the front lines, is divided into bomber, reconnaissance, and fighter plane squadrons. Before taking off, the pilots responsible for bombing enemy lines load their planes with shells, which are dropped from the aircraft's underside. The reconnaissance units, equipped with wireless radios, bring cameras on board. These cameras can take pictures at an altitude of 2,000 m. On the ground, the photos are developed and printed in a photo lab-car, then analyzed using a map. As for the fighter planes, they attack the enemy with machine guns.

video_description: Ficha pública de Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - Archives françaises du film no European Film Gateway. | player EFG sinalizado no HTML, sem fonte de mídia materializada na coleta estática | título alternativo: Ginette's Turco | gênero: Short film | duração: 00:07:08 | coleção: Centre national du cinéma et de l'image animée - CNC | direção: Unknown | produtora: Eclair | cor: Tinted / Toned / Hand coloured | som: Without sound | direitos: In Copyright | ver também: http://lise.cnc.fr/Internet/ARemplir/parcours/EFG1914/pages_FR/187369.html | Little Ginette learns from a newspaper that Amama, a "Turco" (a soldier from the French colonies), has been decorated for his superb feats in battle. In admiration of his incredible bravery, the young girl decides to send him one of her dolls, accompanied by a letter stating that she would like to marry him when she grows up. Injured during another heroic exploit, Amama is brought to a hospital in Paris. Ginette, having heard the sad news, goes to his bedside. She cries out when she sees that he is black then, changing her mind, approaches him and gives him a kiss. Amama spends his recovery at his young guardian's home.
